name: Udalaitz, Erdikoatxa and Besaide — circular from Udala
name_original: Udalaitz, Erdikoatxa y Besaide — circular desde Udala
region_intro: Hiking around Arrasate / Mondragón offers diverse terrain within a mountainous landscape, shaped by the Deba River. The region features prominent peaks such as Udalaitz (1,117 meters), Murugain (778 meters), and Kurtzetxiki (532 meters), providing varied elevation gains. Trails often traverse lush forests and open mountain areas, connecting rural neighborhoods and natural features. The area is characterized by its significant natural value and well-established trail networks. Best hiking trails around Arrasate / Mondragón The most popular hiking route is Udalaitz, Erdikoatxa and Besaide — circular from Udala, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends multiple peaks, offering expansive views of the surrounding mountains. Another top favourite among local hikers is Besaide Monument – Udala Neighborhood and Church loop from Udala, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.2 km) path. This loop explores the Udala neighborhood and features the historic Besaide Monument. Local hikers also love the Udalatx Oriental Summit – Udalaitz Peak (1,119 m) loop from Udala, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. This difficult route provides access to the Udalaitz peak. Hiking around Arrasate / Mondragón is defined by its mountainous landscape, prominent peaks, and diverse trail networks. The region offers routes suitable for various ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Arrasate / Mondragón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Arrasate / Mondragón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the typical terrain and elevation changes like on hikes around Arrasate / Mondragón?
A: The hiking trails around Arrasate / Mondragón are characterized by a mountainous landscape with significant elevation changes. You'll encounter prominent peaks like Udalaitz (1,117 meters) and Murugain (778 meters). Routes often traverse lush green forests, rocky sections, and open mountain areas, providing a diverse hiking experience.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for different difficulty levels?
A: Yes, Arrasate / Mondragón offers a variety of trails catering to different abilities. There are 5 easy routes, 36 moderate routes, and 22 difficult routes. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6973889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besaide Monument – Udala Neighborhood and Church loop from Udala&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, while &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037542140/udalaitz-erdikoatxa-and-besaide-circular-from-udala" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Udalaitz, Erdikoatxa and Besaide — circular from Udala&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see on hikes in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural features. You can find majestic mountains like Udalaitz, which offers expansive views. Santa Bárbara Park, near the urban center, provides magnificent panoramic views of the town. You might also encounter caves, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eye of Aitzulo&lt;/a&gt;, or see the mythical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anboto (1,331 m)&lt;/a&gt; mountain in the distance.

Q: Are there any interesting caves or historical sites along the trails?
A: Yes, the area features several interesting sites. You can explore caves like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eye of Aitzulo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3829487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aitzorrotz Cave&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1118692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besaide Monument&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6973889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besaide Monument – Udala Neighborhood and Church loop from Udala&lt;/a&gt; route, and ancient trees in Monterrón Park.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Arrasate / Mondragón?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't provided, there are 5 easy routes and 36 moderate routes that could be suitable for families depending on their fitness levels. The well-maintained and signposted trails make navigation easier for all. Parks like Monterrón and Santa Bárbara also offer accessible green spaces near the urban center.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: The natural trails around Arrasate / Mondragón are generally welcoming for outdoor activities. While there isn't specific information regarding dog restrictions on all trails, it's common for hikers to bring dogs on mountain paths in the region. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations or signage you may encounter.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Arrasate / Mondragón?
A: Hikes in the area vary significantly in duration. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6973889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besaide Monument – Udala Neighborhood and Church loop from Udala&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours 20 minutes. More challenging routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037542140/udalaitz-erdikoatxa-and-besaide-circular-from-udala" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Udalaitz, Erdikoatxa and Besaide — circular from Udala&lt;/a&gt; can take over 4 hours.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Arrasate / Mondragón offers several circular routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037542140/udalaitz-erdikoatxa-and-besaide-circular-from-udala" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Udalaitz, Erdikoatxa and Besaide — circular from Udala&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6973889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besaide Monument – Udala Neighborhood and Church loop from Udala&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23823977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkulu Reservoir – View of Urkulu Reservoir loop from Arrasate / Mondragón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which hikes offer the best panoramic views?
A: For stunning panoramic views, consider routes that ascend the prominent peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037542140/udalaitz-erdikoatxa-and-besaide-circular-from-udala" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Udalaitz, Erdikoatxa and Besaide — circular from Udala&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, ascends multiple peaks and offers expansive mountain vistas. Santa Bárbara Park also provides excellent views over the town.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Arrasate / Mondragón?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the challenge of ascending prominent peaks, and the well-maintained, signposted trails that make exploring the region's natural beauty enjoyable.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are 5 easy routes available in Arrasate / Mondragón. While specific beginner routes aren't detailed, these easier options typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them ideal for those new to hiking or looking for a more relaxed walk. The well-maintained trails ensure a pleasant experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Arrasate / Mondragón?
A: The mountainous landscape of Arrasate / Mondragón is enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for hiking. Summer can also be great, especially for higher altitude trails, while winter might offer unique, snow-dusted views on some routes.
tips: Neighborhood of the municipality of Mondragón with a rural atmosphere that is located at the foot of the Udalaitz mountain, becoming a starting point for excursionists.

A fabulous environment that gives mountain biking the right kick!
